titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/247

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=223 status=live modified=2021-04-05 09:35:37.563782+00 | translation moa=222 status=live modified=2021-04-05 09:35:34.111333+00

div.p: C Y R O G R A P H U M

p.cl:
Hec est conuentio facta Inter Dominum . W . abatem ⁊ conuentum sancte crucis de Edinburg ex una parte ⁊ Walterum
Magistrum ⁊ fratres de Torphean . ex altera parte de contouersia que uertebatur inter eos ⁊ coram iudicibus delegatis diu
uentilabatur super decimis ⁊ ouentionibus de Ogilfas . quod predicti abbas ⁊ conuentus consensu ⁊ autoritate iudicum
delegatorum in presencia domini . W . de Bosch’ . domini regis cancellarii ⁊ aliorum prudentum uiros . pro bono pacis concesserunt
predictis fratribus omnes decimationes ⁊ ouentiones ecclesiasticas quas ipsi habebant ⁊ percipere solebant de terra de Ogilfas .
Predicti uero fratres tenebunt ⁊ inperpetuum possidebunt prenominatas decimas libere ⁊ quiete plenarie ⁊ honorifice reddendo inde
abbati ⁊ conuentui sancte crucis de Edenburg singulis annis quatuor Marcas argenti . duas ad pentecost’ ⁊ duas ad festum
sancti martini.

Ut autem hec conuentio rata ⁊ inconcussa permaneat in perpetuum signa iudicum de predicta causa delegatorum
⁊ capituli conuentus sancti andree ⁊ capituli domus hospitalis lundon’ . presenti scripto in testimonium ⁊ confirmationem
huius conuentionis apponuntur ne in irritum possit reuocari.

div.p: C Y R O G R A P H

p.cl: This is the agreement made between the lord abbot, W., and the convent of Holyrood in Edinburgh on the one side and Walter the Master, and the brethren of Torphichen on the other side, concerning the dispute which arose between them and was aired for a long while before judges delegate, over the teinds and revenues from Ogilface, that the aforesaid abbot and convent, with the consent and authority of the judges delegate, in the presence of the lord William del Bois, chancellor of the lord king, and other judicious men, for the sake of good peace, granted to the aforesaid brethen all the teinds and ecclesiastical revenues that they themselves used to have and were accustomed to receive from the land of Ogilface. The aforesaid brethren, however, shall hold and in perpetuity possess the aforenamed teinds freely and quietly, fully and honourably, by rendering thereupon to the abbot and convent of Holyrood in Edinburgh each year four merks of silver, two at Pentecost and two at the feast of Saint Martin.

In order that this agreement remain sure and unbroken in perpetuity the seals of the judges delegate of the aforesaid case and the chapter of the convent of St Andrews and the chapter of the hospital of London [Clerkenwell?] are set to the present document in testimony and confirmation of this agreement lest it could be revoked as being invalid.
